data.title: Bitcoin Bounces To $88,000 As Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin Trade Sideways
data.description:

data.title: The market capitalization of the Memecoin sector has fallen 65% in one year to $35 billion.
data.description: According to CoinMarketCap data, the market capitalization of the Memecoin sector fell to $35 billion on December 19, 2025, its lowest level of the year, a 65% drop from the approximately $100 billion valuation during the Christmas period last year. As of Friday, the sector's market capitalization had recovered to approximately $36 billion. The annual trading volume of the Memecoin sector declined by 72% to $3.05 trillion, influenced by retail investor trends shifting towards less speculative assets. CoinGecko points out that political narratives in 2024 drove the sector's growth, but subsequent price declines in tokens associated with political figures and insider activity led to a shift in market sentiment towards skepticism. Furthermore, the NFT sector's valuation also fell to a 2025 low of $2.5 billion in December, a 72% drop from its January peak of $9.2 billion. CryptoSlam data shows that the number of weekly active NFT sellers fell below 100,000 for the first time since April 2021. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Saylor Reveals What Will Drive Bitcoin Price To New ATHs – It’s Not What You Think
data.description:
Bitcoin price dynamics heading into the next market cycle are being reframed by Michael Saylor, who argues that the forces capable of pushing Bitcoin to new all-time highs have little to do with speculation, retail enthusiasm, or ETF-driven flows. Instead, Saylor’s outlook positions Bitcoin price appreciation as the outcome of a deeper structural transition that is unfolding quietly within the banking system. Michael Saylor On Bitcoin Price’s Structural Shift As the market looks toward 2026, Michael Saylor’s thesis on Bitcoin price action focuses on a structural shift away from trader-driven dynamics toward regulated financial institutions, a transition that could fundamentally reshape how capital engages with Bitcoin at scale. For most of its history, Bitcoin price discovery has been dominated by cyclical trading behavior, leverage, and sentiment-driven momentum
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/bitcoin/saylor-drive-bitcoin-price/

data.title: Japan Considers Tax Reform for Cryptocurrency Income
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, Japan's Liberal Democratic Party and the Japan Innovation Party have proposed classifying virtual currencies as financial products that aid in public asset accumulation in their 2026 fiscal year tax reform outline. The proposal suggests applying a separate taxation system to cryptocurrency income, similar to that of stocks and investment trusts.

The outline specifies that spot trading, derivatives trading, and ETFs involving virtual currencies would be subject to separate taxation. Additionally, losses from cryptocurrency trading could be carried forward for three years. Financial products investing in virtual currencies are also included in the proposal. However, the outline does not clearly address the taxation details for NFTs or reward-based transactions such as staking and lending. It also mentions that future gains from virtual currencies may be subject to taxation when transferred overseas.

data.title: Japan's tax reform is considering adopting a separate taxation system for virtual currencies, supporting the carry-forward of losses over three years.
data.description: On December 19th, Japan's Liberal Democratic Party and the Japan Innovation Party released a tax reform outline for fiscal year 2026. The outline proposes classifying virtual currencies as financial products that contribute to public asset accumulation and is considering applying a separate tax system to virtual currency income, similar to that for stocks and investment trusts. According to the outline, spot trading, derivatives trading, and ETFs of virtual currencies will be taxed separately, and losses from virtual currency trading can be carried forward for three years. Furthermore, financial products investing in virtual currencies are also included. Currently, the plan does not explicitly mention tax details for NFTs or reward-based transactions such as staking and lending. The outline also mentions that future earnings from virtual currencies may be subject to taxation when transferred overseas. (CoinPost)

data.title: Russia’s Top Exchanges Set to Launch Regulated Crypto Trading by 2026
data.description:
Russia’s top exchanges, Moscow and St. Petersburg, are preparing to launch regulated cryptocurrency trading by mid-2026 under a new legal framework targeting highly qualified investors. The exchanges are building infrastructure and compliance measures, signaling a major shift in Russia’s financial landscape toward integrating digital assets responsibly and securely. The post Russia’s Top Exchanges Set to Launch Regulated Crypto Trading by 2026 first appeared on The Shib Daily
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/12/26/russias-top-exchanges-set-to-launch-regulated-crypto-trading-by-2026/
